result.summary: Mapped Comet’s public developer surfaces: Comet REST API and CLI authenticated by a Comet API key, Opik REST API authenticated by an Opik API key, and a public streamable-http docs MCP server at /docs/opik/_mcp/server.

comet_api_key.label: Comet API key
comet_api_key.setup: Create or copy your Comet API key from [Comet REST API overview](https://www.comet.com/docs/v2/api-and-sdk/rest-api/overview/). The docs say to go to account settings, click your avatar, select **Account settings**, then open the **API keys** tab. You can then use that key directly in requests, or run `comet login` / set `COMET_API_KEY` for CLI and SDK usage as described in [Configure the Comet SDK](https://www.comet.com/docs/v2/guides/experiment-management/configure-sdk/) and the [CLI reference](https://www.comet.com/docs/v2/api-and-sdk/command-line/reference/).

opik_api_key.label: Opik API key
opik_api_key.setup: Create or copy your Opik API key from your Opik workspace settings or user menu, as referenced in [Opik FAQ](https://www.comet.com/docs/opik/faq). For local/self-hosted installs this may be unnecessary, but for Opik Cloud the SDK reference notes an `api_key` and defaults the API URL to `https://www.comet.com/opik/api` in [Opik Python SDK reference](https://www.comet.com/docs/opik/python-sdk-reference/Opik.html).
